preamble.summary: Jacob journeys to Bethel and fulfills his vow to tithe. He tithes his sons, counting from the youngest, and the tenth falls on Levi — thus Levi is 'tithed to the Lord' and consecrated for priesthood. Levi receives a priestly ordination vision. Rachel gives birth to Benjamin and dies in childbirth.
preamble.remarkable: The tithing of sons to determine the priestly tribe is a brilliant narrative invention. By counting from Benjamin upward, the tenth son is Levi — making his priestly selection appear to be the result of mathematical providence rather than arbitrary choice.
preamble.friction: The tithing mechanism for selecting Levi has no basis in any other source. The celebration of Tabernacles at Bethel is anachronistic by Pentateuchal standards, but Jubilees consistently presents patriarchs as observing Mosaic law before Sinai.
preamble.connections: Genesis 28:20-22 (Jacob's tithe vow at Bethel); Genesis 35:16-20 (Rachel's death); Leviticus 23:33-43 (Festival of Tabernacles); Testament of Levi 2-5 (Levi's heavenly vision).

verses.text_reference: And he abode that night at Bethel, and Levi dreamed that they had ordained and made him the priest of the Most High God, him and his sons for ever.
verses.rendering: He stayed that night at Bethel. Levi dreamed that he had been ordained and appointed as priest of the Most High God — he and his sons forever.
translator_notes: Levi's ordination dream parallels the heavenly ascent in the Testament of Levi.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob rose early in the morning, on the fourteenth of this month, and he gave a tithe of all that came with him, both of men and of cattle, both of gold and every vessel and garment.
verses.rendering: Jacob rose early in the morning on the fourteenth of this month and gave a tithe of everything that had come with him — people and livestock, gold and every vessel and garment.

verses.text_reference: And in those days Rachel became pregnant with her son Benjamin. And Jacob counted his sons from him upwards and Levi fell to the portion of the Lord, and his father clothed him in the garments of the priesthood and filled his hands.
verses.rendering: In those days Rachel was pregnant with her son Benjamin. Jacob counted his sons, beginning from the youngest and going upward, and Levi fell as the Lord's portion. His father clothed him in the garments of the priesthood and consecrated him.
translator_notes: 'Filled his hands' (mille' yad) is the technical term for priestly ordination found in Exodus 28:41.

key_terms.note: The technical ordination formula — Hebrew: mille' yad — meaning to consecrate for priestly service

verses.text_reference: And he tithed again all the clean animals, and made a burnt offering, and a meat offering, and a thank offering, and a drink offering.
verses.rendering: He tithed again all the clean animals and made a burnt offering, a grain offering, a thanksgiving offering, and a drink offering.

verses.text_reference: And he presented a thank offering to the Lord God who had saved him.
verses.rendering: He presented a thanksgiving offering to the Lord God who had saved him and was watching over him.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob kept there the feast of Tabernacles, and he made a tabernacle and kept the feast seven days.
verses.rendering: Jacob observed the Festival of Tabernacles there. He built a booth and celebrated the festival for seven days, rejoicing with abundant gladness.
translator_notes: The patriarchal observance of Sukkot before the Sinai legislation is characteristic of Jubilees' theology: Torah is eternal.

verses.text_reference: And he blessed the Creator of all things who had preserved him.
verses.rendering: He blessed the Creator of all things, who had preserved him and delivered him from every evil.

verses.text_reference: And he built there an altar, and he poured a libation upon it.
verses.rendering: He built an altar there and poured a libation on it. He offered a burnt offering on it before God.

verses.text_reference: And he called the name of that place Bethel, in the land of Canaan.
verses.rendering: He named that place Bethel — the House of God — in the land of Canaan.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob made a feast on that day, and he and all his sons ate and drank.
verses.rendering: Jacob held a feast that day. He and all his sons ate and drank and rejoiced.

verses.text_reference: And when the days of the feast were finished, he set out from thence to the south.
verses.rendering: When the days of the festival were finished, he departed and headed south toward Ephrath.

verses.text_reference: And on the way Rachel travailed, and she gave birth, and her labour was hard.
verses.rendering: On the way, Rachel went into labor. She gave birth, but her labor was difficult.

verses.text_reference: And it came to pass, as her soul was departing — for she died — she named him Ben-oni; but his father called him Benjamin.
verses.rendering: As her life was slipping away — for she was dying — she named him Ben-oni, 'Son of My Sorrow.' But his father called him Benjamin, on the eleventh of the eighth month, in the first year of the sixth week of the forty-fifth jubilee.
translator_notes: Rachel's death in childbirth is one of the most sorrowful moments in Genesis (35:16-20).

verses.text_reference: And Rachel died there and she was buried in the land of Ephrath, the same is Bethlehem.
verses.rendering: Rachel died there and was buried in the territory of Ephrath — that is, Bethlehem. Jacob set up a pillar on Rachel's grave.

verses.text_reference: And Isaac sent to Jacob his son ten sheep, and two oxen, and two asses.
verses.rendering: Isaac sent to his son Jacob ten sheep, two oxen, two donkeys, and a female servant.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob went and came to his father Isaac on the new moon of the tenth month.
verses.rendering: Jacob traveled and came to his father Isaac at the new moon of the tenth month.

verses.text_reference: And Isaac blessed the God of his father Abraham.
verses.rendering: Isaac blessed the God of his father Abraham, who had not withdrawn his mercy and his blessing from the offspring of his servant Isaac.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob gave to Isaac and to Rebecca a present of oil and wine and food.
verses.rendering: Jacob gave his father Isaac and his mother Rebekah a gift of oil, wine, and food.

verses.text_reference: And they ate and drank, and Isaac blessed the Most High God.
verses.rendering: They ate and drank, and Isaac blessed the Most High God who had provided them with good things.

verses.text_reference: And Isaac knew that the Lord had blessed him in all his works.
verses.rendering: Isaac recognized that the Lord had blessed him in all his undertakings, and he gave thanks.

verses.text_reference: And Isaac blessed Jacob, and Jacob went forth from him in peace.
verses.rendering: Isaac blessed Jacob, and Jacob departed from him in peace and joy.

verses.text_reference: And Esau also came to Isaac, and he blessed him.
verses.rendering: Esau also came to Isaac, who blessed him. Then Esau went on his way.

verses.text_reference: And in those days Leah bore a daughter, and Jacob called her name Dinah.
verses.rendering: In those days, Leah bore a daughter. Jacob named her Dinah.

verses.text_reference: And after this Leah bore no more.
verses.rendering: After this, Leah bore no more children. Rachel bore Joseph, her firstborn. Bilhah bore Dan and Naphtali, and Zilpah bore Gad and Asher.

verses.text_reference: And these are the sons of Jacob and their birth-order.
verses.rendering: These are the sons of Jacob and the order of their births, as they were born to him in Mesopotamia.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob was seventy-seven years old when he went forth from Canaan.
verses.rendering: Jacob was seventy-seven years old when he departed from the land of Canaan, from Beer-sheba.

verses.text_reference: And Bilhah and Zilpah he took as concubines, not as wives.
verses.rendering: Bilhah and Zilpah he took as concubines, not as wives, for Leah and Rachel were his wives.
translator_notes: Jubilees carefully distinguishes between wives and concubines — a legal distinction relevant to inheritance.

verses.text_reference: And he came to Bethel on the first of the month.
verses.rendering: He arrived at Bethel on the first of the month of this week and named the place Bethel.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob gave there a tithe of everything and sanctified it to the Lord.
verses.rendering: There Jacob gave a tithe of everything and consecrated it to the Lord. The place became holy.

verses.text_reference: And he saw in a vision of the night an angel with seven tablets in his hands.
verses.rendering: He saw a vision in the night: an angel descended from heaven carrying seven tablets in his hands. He gave them to Jacob, who read them and learned everything written on them.
translator_notes: The seven tablets contain predestined history — a concept central to Jubilees' deterministic theology.

key_terms.note: Heavenly tablets containing the predestined future of Jacob's line

verses.text_reference: And he showed him all that was written, and said: 'Do not build this place into an eternal sanctuary.'
verses.rendering: The angel showed him everything written on the tablets and said: Do not build this place into an eternal sanctuary, and do not settle here. Go to the house of your father Abraham.
translator_notes: The prohibition against making Bethel a permanent sanctuary subtly supports Jerusalem's exclusive claim.

verses.text_reference: For in Egypt thou shalt die in peace, and in this land thou shalt be buried with honour.
verses.rendering: In Egypt you will die in peace, and in this land you will be buried with honor in the tomb of your fathers.

verses.text_reference: And fear not, for as thou hast seen and read it, thus shall it all be.
verses.rendering: Do not be afraid. As you have seen and read, so it will all come to pass. Write down everything just as you have seen and read it.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob said: 'Lord, how can I remember all that I have read and seen?' And he said: 'I will bring all things to thy remembrance.'
verses.rendering: Jacob said: Lord, how can I remember everything I have read and seen? The angel replied: I will bring all of it back to your memory.
